attributes.action: Reform pet insurance to make cover fairer and more affordable
attributes.additional_details: Many pet owners are paying more for less cover and feel trapped. Annual premiums continue to increase substantially despite no claims being made, yet switching insurers often results in broad pre-existing condition exclusions. A one-off ear infection can lead to exclusions for ears, skin and paws, while a short-term gastric illness may exclude future digestive issues regardless of cause. Pet owners deserve stronger consumer protections and a fairer insurance market.
attributes.background: Require the Government and FCA to review and reform pet insurance regulations, including rules on pre-existing condition exclusions, excessive premium increases, co-payments, loyalty penalties and consumer protections.

rejection.details:
we're not exactly sure what you mean by "make it fairer and more affordable". We currently have these petitions open which you may wish to sign .... 

https://petition.parliament.uk/petitions/755151
https://petition.parliament.uk/petitions/759667
